text: The average marks obtained by a class in an examination were calculated as 30.8. options.B: Both (1) and (3) are true.
options.C: Only (3) is true.

text: “I put the brown paper in my pocket along with the chalks, and possibly other things.  I suppose every one   must have reflected how primeval and how poetical are the  things that one carries in one’s pocket: the pocket - knife, for instance the type of all  human tools, the infant of the sword. Once I planned to write a book of poems  entirely about the things in my   pocket. But I found it would be too long: and the age  of the great epics is past.”  (From G.K. Chesterton’s “A Piece of Chalk”)  Based only on the information provided in the above passage, which one of the  following statements is true?

options.A: The author of the passage carries a mirror in his pocket to reflect upon things.
options.B: The author of the passage had decided to write a poem on epics.
options.C: The pocket - knife is described as the infant of the   sword.
options.D: Epics are described as too inconvenient to write.  7   of   64

options.D: ∀ 𝑥 ∃ 𝑦 [ 𝑚𝑜𝑡 ℎ 𝑒𝑟 ( 𝑦 ,   𝑥 )   ∧   ¬ ∃ 𝑧 ( 𝑛𝑜𝑡𝑒𝑞 ( 𝑧 ,   𝑦 )   ∧   𝑚𝑜𝑡 ℎ 𝑒𝑟 ( 𝑧 ,   𝑥 ) ) ]  49   of   64
